title: Settle & Pour: Sedimentation + Decantation
activity: Topic M2.S4.T10 / Fig. 9.9 — Removing tea leaves without a strainer (sedimentation, then decantation)
description: A tap-and-slide Class 6 guided experiment on the two-step separation sequence for a heavier insoluble solid in a liquid. In Explore (learn) mode the student sees a pan of tea with the leaves mixed through it, predicts what a fast tilt will do, presses 'Wait' to watch the leaves sediment to the bottom (SEDIMENTATION), then drags a tilt slider to pour the clear tea into a cup (DECANTATION). The pour control is physically honest: a slow tilt keeps the leaves resting and only clear tea flows out, but a fast yank lifts the settled leaves so the cup fills with cloudy tea — the built-in failure case that teaches 'tilt slowly'. The wrap states the rule (sedimentation first, then decantation; works only on INSOLUBLE solids). Quiz (practice) mode is a gamified round on the step order, the two names, the fast-tilt failure, the insoluble rule (salt never settles), and the rice-washing real-world case, with a result popup (score count-up, stars, confetti) and a wrong-answer concept review. No drag-drop of cards anywhere — only taps and one tilt slider.

additionalProps.feedbackMessage: Wait for the leaves to settle before tilting. Tilt slowly.

gradeLevel.description: Selects the tier preset. 6 = heaviest gamification, story framing, biggest text, slowest/most dramatic settle, 70-particle confetti + 5 stars. 7 = moderate. 8 = light/formal. This tool is authored for Class 6.

themeColor.description: Accent hint. The tool uses the Singularity palette internally — header purple, SEDIMENTATION tag purple #4A4DC9, DECANTATION tag + CTAs orange #FF7212.

enabledModes.description: Modes shown in the selector (Explore / Quiz).

showModeSelector.description: Show the Explore/Quiz toggle in the header.

showNavigation.description: Show Back/Next in Explore. Auto-hidden when filterSteps freezes the tool on a single screen.

showPlayPause.description: Reserved; this interactive tool advances via Next/Back and in-stage taps, not host autoplay.

filterSteps.description: Freeze the Explore flow on ONE screen and hide the nav. Accepts a single-element array of S1..S4: S1=See the mix, S2=Predict, S3=Wait/sedimentation, S4=Tilt/decantation (slow = clear, fast = cloudy; ends with the rule + quiz CTA). Empty = full free-running flow.

autoPlayDuration.description: Host auto-advance hint, in ms. Ignored for actual stepping — the tool calls setStopAutoNext(true) because every screen needs interaction.

panState.description: Initial settle state of the pan when the tool mounts. 'mixed' = leaves suspended throughout (start of the experiment), 'settling' = part-way settled, 'settled' = leaves already resting at the bottom (clear tea on top). Use 'settled' to drop a learner straight onto the decantation step.

tiltAngle.description: Initial tilt of the pan/pour control in degrees (0 = upright, 60 = fully tipped). Pouring begins past ~16°. Useful to present a frozen 'mid-pour' illustration.

cupContents.description: Initial contents of the cup. 'clear' = a successful slow decantation has already half-filled the cup with clear tea; 'cloudy' = a fast tilt has poured disturbed, leaf-laden tea (the failure outcome). Lets an agent open the tool on a worked example.

showHints.description: Show the small inline coaching hints under the slider and pan.

feedbackMessage.default: Wait for the leaves to settle before tilting. Tilt slowly.
feedbackMessage.description: One-line coaching message shown under the pan on the See-the-mix and Test-fast-tilt screens.

predictionPrompt.default: Will tilting FAST pour clear tea or cloudy tea?
predictionPrompt.description: Question shown on the Predict screen (S2).

liquidLabel.description: Re-theme the liquid. Pair with solidLabel/colours to reuse the same mechanic for other sediment→decant cases, e.g. 'muddy water', 'rice water'.

solidLabel.description: Name of the heavier insoluble solid that settles, e.g. 'tea leaves', 'mud', 'rice grains', 'sand'.

liquidColor.description: Hex colour of the still-mixed liquid (clears toward an internal lighter tint once settled).

solidColor.description: Hex colour of the settled solid band and the suspended-particle cloud.

modes.label: Explore
modes.description: See the mixed pan → predict the fast-tilt effect → press Wait to watch sedimentation → drag the tilt slider to decant slowly (a fast yank goes cloudy — the built-in failure case), then the rule wrap and quiz CTA.

modes.label: Quiz
modes.description: Gamified 4-question round on step order, the two names, the fast-tilt failure, the insoluble rule, and rice-washing → result popup → wrong-answer concept review → play again with a fresh shuffle.

learn.title: See the mixed pan
learn.description: A pan of tea with leaves mixed throughout; a cup waits alongside. Sets the problem: get clear tea, leave the leaves behind.

learn.title: Predict the tilt effect
learn.description: Student predicts whether a fast tilt pours clear or cloudy tea (tap-select). Gate: a prediction is made.

learn.title: Wait for sedimentation
learn.description: Press 'Wait' to watch the heavier insoluble leaves sink and settle into a dark band at the bottom; a SEDIMENTATION tag appears. Gate: settling completes.

learn.title: Tilt slowly to decant
learn.description: Drag the tilt slider; a slow tilt pours clear tea into the cup while the leaves stay put (DECANTATION tag). A fast yank lifts the settled leaves so the cup goes cloudy — the built-in failure case that teaches 'tilt slowly'. Closes with the sedimentation-then-decantation rule and a 'Take the Quiz' CTA.

practice.title: Quiz round
practice.description: 4 randomized questions: which step comes first; name the pour-off step (decantation); the fast-tilt outcome; will salt settle (insoluble rule); rice-washing as decantation. Silent play.

practice.title: Result
practice.description: Score count-up, 5 stars, 70-particle confetti, tier encouragement, CTAs.

practice.title: Review
practice.description: Wrong-answer cards with cause-and-effect explanations; prev/next; play again.

interactionNotes: Tap/click + ONE tilt slider — no drag-and-drop of cards (house rule; robust on mobile for young students).

The pan/cup is a single responsive SVG side-view scene driven by four numbers: settle (0..1), tilt (deg), poured (0..1), disturb (0..1). The maths is physically honest so the picture always reads true.

Failure case is emergent, not scripted: tilt SPEED (deg/sec) above a threshold raises 'disturb', which lifts the settled band and turns both the pour stream and the cup cloudy. Easing off lets it slowly re-settle.
Sedimentation animation duration scales by tier (slowest at Class 6 for drama).
filterSteps S1..S4 freezes Explore on a single screen and hides Back/Next, for embedding one moment at a time alongside a lesson.
Calls setStopAutoNext(true) and reports setStepDetails on every step/phase change.
Responsive from 320px; touch targets 56px at Class 6; scene caps at 560px wide and scales down with the container.
instructions_for_student: Wait for the leaves to settle, then tilt slowly to pour clear tea. Try a fast tilt too — see what goes wrong.
